records.ety: [L. recurrere; pref. re- re- + currere to run. See Current.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Recurred (-k?rd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Recurring.]

defs: To come back; to return again or repeatedly; to come again to mind.
quotes: When any word has been used to signify an idea, the old idea will recur in the mind when the word is heard.

defs: To occur at a stated interval, or according to some regular rule; as, the fever will recur to-night.
To resort; to have recourse; to go for help.
quotes: If, to avoid succession in eternal existence, they recur to the “punctum stans” of the schools, they will thereby very little help us to a more positive idea of infinite duration.
